lines: &1 (left ) : {n : nonce, c1 : bytes, t, t0 : poly_out, a : associated_data,
p1 : message, nap : nonce * associated_data * message,
p, p0 : plaintext, c, c0 : ciphertext, lt : tag list}
&2 (right) : {n : nonce, c1 : bytes, t, t0 : poly_out, a : associated_data,
p1 : message, nap : nonce * associated_data * message,
p, p0 : plaintext, c, c0 : ciphertext, lt : tag list}
inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2}
exists (tt : tag * tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2)) /\
c1 <@ EncRnd.cc(n, p1)                     ( 4--)  c1 <@ EncRnd.cc(n, p1)
map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4)     (   -)    map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4)
c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc)               (   -)            c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc)
size lt <= qdec) {                     (   -)      size lt <= qdec) {
ROout.set((n, C.ofintd 0), witness)        (10--)  ROout.set((n, C.ofintd 0), witness)
UFCMA.log.[n <- (a, c1, t)]              (   -)    UFCMA.log.[n <- (a, c1, t)]
Mem.log <- Mem.log.[c0 <- p0]              (13--)  Mem.log <- Mem.log.[c0 <- p0]
BNR.lenc <- p.`1 :: BNR.lenc               (15--)  BNR.lenc <- p.`1 :: BNR.lenc
inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2}

navigation.confidence_reason: proof-state analysis has a typed named-call handle and current call-site/frontier facts for this proof state.

named_handles.frontier_status_detail: matching call is part of a same-concrete-procedure pair; treat named cross-procedure lemmas as lower-priority until the local synchronized region has been accounted for

action.tactic_shape: seq 4 4 : <invariant preserving the call lemma postcondition>.
action.reason: the matching call pair exists before the last-call frontier; split the sequence at that pair before using a call lemma

lemma_precondition_coverage.strategy_boundary: Static coverage only: it tells the prover what facts the call will need, but it does not prove those facts or rank the proof strategy by lemma name.

navigation.effect: This panel summarizes current call-site and frontier facts; it does not commit a tactic.
limitations: A displayed tactic shape is evidence about the current frontier, not proof by itself.
Frontier blockers are present for at least one named handle.
Some named-call value parameters require explicit binding.

navigation.confidence_reason: The current pRHL goal exposes a bad-event/instrumented branch together with sampling or one-sided suffix evidence; this is the shape where experts use `case`, `rcondt/rcondf`, `swap`, indexed `wp`, `conseq`, or one-sided `rnd` before local SMT. The fast-track tactic is schematic, so confidence is capped until the current view or an inspect result supplies concrete terms.
navigation.why_now: current pRHL state looks past the main opener: the useful next work is local branch/suffix surgery, not changing proof family.

preconditions: Use only when the visible goal has a concrete guard controlling the local branch.
After the case split, use `rcondt`/`rcondf` for forced branches, `swap` for statement order, indexed `wp` for suffixes, `conseq` for a smaller post, or `rnd{1}`/`rnd{2}` for one-sided samples.
If the guard fact needed for `rcondt`/`rcondf` is not in the precondition, treat that as an invariant repair signal.
Probe the smallest local block; do not fresh-restart just because this surgery is syntax-sensitive.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: Branch-specific pRHL residuals where forced-condition, swap, indexed-wp, one-sided-random, or conseq steps can be checked.

anti_routes.reason: This is a branch-local/suffix alignment problem; a fresh restart throws away useful opener and invariant work.
One side has sampling or instrumentation before the suffix; use `rcond`, `swap`, or one-sided `rnd` before plain `wp`.
`sim` is usually too late/too strong before the guard and suffix shape are aligned.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for swap, rcondt, rcondf, wp, conseq, rnd, or eager
inspect align or goal_info for statement indexes before retrying
use undo_to_checkpoint {} and choose the checkpoint before the call/loop invariant that should carry the missing guard or size fact
inspect lemma_hints or lookup_symbol for the local size/nth/bad-event lemma
downgrade this surgery route only after a smaller prefix and relevant tactic_forms fail

navigation.confidence_reason: The current view exposes a live call frontier for a pRHL/procedure state.
navigation.why_now: current pRHL/procedure frontier has both_sides_at_call evidence; choose a structural step before local closure.

preconditions: Use only on the current pRHL/procedure frontier.
If the first instructions are not aligned, consume or align setup before branch tactics.
When one side has setup before an aligned branch, prefer indexed `sp i j` from the alignment counts.
Probe first; commit only the accepted structural step or prefix.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: Call preservation obligations, or a concrete invariant gap.

anti_routes.reason: first instructions are left `call` and right `call`, so branch frontiers are not aligned.
Inlining everything erases live call handles before the call route is checked.
Plain `sim.` needs EasyCrypt to infer the equality relation. At a pRHL frontier with visible call/setup structure, first make the relation explicit with call, seq/wp, alignment, or conseq.
anti_routes.usable_when: `sim.` becomes a good short tactic when the frontiers are already synchronized or after `conseq`/call/structural steps expose an explicit equality relation for the remaining instructions.

anti_routes.reason: Procedure/frontier goals usually need structural exposure before pure SMT.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect goal_info or align for first-instruction details
inspect call_subgoals/subgoal_gap; if generated obligations lack facts that should have been preserved, use undo_to_checkpoint {} before the call or loop invariant
lookup_symbol before applying non-trivial call/branch lemmas
downgrade this route and choose another current-view navigation item

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.guidance: Inspect bad-event candidate map: UFCMA.bad1 (postcondition), UFCMA.cbad1 (postcondition), UFCMA_l.lbad1 (postcondition), inv_lbad1 (postcondition).
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

moves.title: Invariant-call route
moves.applicability: This is a `call (_: Inv)` route template, not a complete tactic; fill in the invariant expression from the current goal before probing or committing it.
moves.runnable_status: Not yet; replace `Inv` with a concrete EasyCrypt invariant before probing or committing this tactic.
moves.needs_frame: Postcondition shape only — no `={...}` equality frame. Build the `={...}` part from `application_context.write_map` (per candidate field: who writes it — the read-only/preserved fields are safe to equate), then add your coupling before this is a usable invariant.
how_to_complete: Read the current precondition, postcondition, and the two visible call sites; include the state that must be preserved across the adversary or oracle call.
If the call generates oracle obligations, include the global/oracle state needed by the named oracle-equivalence handles visible in the current context.

inspect_if_unsure.why: Shows the live call frontier and any named call or oracle-equivalence handles.
After you have a candidate invariant, previews the obligations and missing facts before changing the proof state.

inspect_if_unsure.name: call
inspect_if_unsure.why: Shows the valid EasyCrypt forms for `call`.

limitations: The invariant is not filled in yet; use the current goal and, if helpful, call-subgoal preview to choose the concrete invariant.

named_handles.why_visible: This named-call shape came from program comparison. Treat it as context until a manager inspection or read-only probe confirms the lemma matches the visible call frontier.
limitations: The tactic shape is hidden until the handle is callable at the current frontier.

named_handles.why_visible: This tactic shape still has placeholders; replace them with concrete EasyCrypt arguments from the current goal before probing or committing it.
limitations: The tactic shape is hidden until the handle is callable at the current frontier.

frontier_blockers.right: 2 setup statement(s): lt <- map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4) (filter (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc); UFCMA_l.lbad1 <- UFCMA_l.lbad1 ++ map (fun (t' : tag) => (t0, t')) lt

rows.left: 2 setup statement(s): lt <- map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4) (filter (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc); UFCMA.bad1 <- UFCMA.bad1 || (t0 \in lt)

rows.proof_read: These statements remain after the live call frontier. Their effects are checked after the call returns; a bare call obligation is checked before these residual statements execute.

residual_frontier.effect: These statements remain after the live call frontier and are checked after the call returns.
limitations: The surface reports suffix structure only; it does not choose whether to use a call, inline, wp, or another local tactic.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; sampling steps are visible inside the frontier; call sites are visible at the frontier; residual statements remain after a call frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: Both sides are at call frontiers; inspect call options or use a call tactic form before guessing an invariant.

rows.left: 3 setup statement(s): p0 <- p; nap <- p0; (n, a, p1) <- nap
rows.right: 3 setup statement(s): p0 <- p; nap <- p0; (n, a, p1) <- nap
rows.proof_read: These are setup statements before the current frontier; preserve the proof-relevant initialized state in later invariants, seq cuts, or calls.
Both sides expose the same call procedure; a named equiv call or an invariant call may apply at this frontier.

rows.left: 2 setup statement(s): lt <- map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4) (filter (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc); UFCMA.bad1 <- UFCMA.bad1 || (t0 \in lt)
rows.right: 2 setup statement(s): lt <- map (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`4) (filter (fun (c2 : ciphertext) => c2.`1 = n) Mem.lc); UFCMA_l.lbad1 <- UFCMA_l.lbad1 ++ map (fun (t' : tag) => (t0, t')) lt
rows.proof_read: These statements remain after the live call frontier. Their effects are checked after the call returns; a bare call obligation is checked before these residual statements execute.
Both sides sample from the same distribution; once the state relation is right, the sampling step can usually be handled by a direct coupling or residual automation.
Read this as a proof-shape alignment row, not as a tactic; choose the next tactic from the semantic difference it exposes.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

right.note: count from frontier alignment; the body parser under-counted the asymmetric (right-only) setup statements (1 parsed)

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 3 3 / wp -3 -3 absorbs the straight-line setup (3 left, 3 right) before the next frontier

branch_guards.rcond_forms: rcondt{1} 7 or rcondf{1} 7 (direction needs guard entailment — yours to decide)
rcondt{2} 7 or rcondf{2} 7 (direction needs guard entailment — yours to decide)

checks: Check whether a typed call/resource handle is live at the current frontier.
Wrapper or frontier exposure can preserve call/resource structure.

cut_position.tactic: seq 5 3 : (={glob A, Mem.log, Mem.lc, BNR.lenc, BNR.ndec, UFCMA.log, UFCMA.cbad1, ROIN.RO.m, ROout.m} /\ inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2} UFCMA.cbad1{2} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (UFCMA.bad1{1} => exists (tt:tag*tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2)).

application_context.how_to_use: Use this section when constructing the concrete invariant inside `call (_: ...)`. This section is not a tactic. It is a checklist for constructing the concrete invariant before using `probe_tactic` or `commit_tactic`.

inspect_if_unsure.why: After you draft a concrete invariant, this preview shows the obligations it creates and can reveal missing facts or extra conjuncts that make the proof harder.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis sees live named call handle(s), but none is currently at EasyCrypt's last-call frontier (equ_cc). `call <lemma>` may fail until wp/seq/targeted transforms expose the matching call as the last statement.

proof-state analysis layer is call_site: 2 call site(s) and 1 callable lemma handle(s) are live (equ_cc). `inline *` is a broad global transformation here and can hide those handles; named calls and targeted inline are route context for comparison.

current_boundary_carries.tactic: seq 5 3 : (={glob A, Mem.log, Mem.lc, BNR.lenc, BNR.ndec, UFCMA.log, UFCMA.cbad1, ROIN.RO.m, ROout.m} /\ inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2} UFCMA.cbad1{2} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (UFCMA.bad1{1} => exists (tt:tag*tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2)).

call (_: ={Mem.log, Mem.lc, BNR.lenc, BNR.ndec, UFCMA.log, UFCMA.cbad1, ROIN.RO.m, ROout.m} /\ inv_lbad1 UFCMA_l.lbad1{2} BNR.lenc{2} UFCMA.log{2} Mem.log{2} Mem.lc{2} UFCMA.cbad1{2} BNR.ndec{2} /\ (UFCMA.bad1{1} => exists (tt:tag*tag), (tt \in UFCMA_l.lbad1{2}) /\ tt.`1 = tt.`2)).

frame_obligation_ledger.interpretation: This ledger compares visible frame facts with the frame facts carried by committed structural boundary assertions. Possible drops are reported only for facts visible in the current local goal evidence. It is evidence about retained facts, not a proof script.

evidence: frame_obligation_ledger reports a frame fact visible in the current local goal evidence
a committed structural boundary does not carry the same frame fact

items: current evidence points at a structural recovery boundary

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The visible frontier contains call sites or named equiv handles may apply.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are at an abstract-adversary `call (_: <inv>)` and want the mechanical glob frame of the invariant before adding your own semantic conjuncts.
ask_manager_for.returns: the mechanical `={glob ...}` frame of the call invariant (shared abstract oracle modules, daemon-confirmed to apply and spawn the obligations). When a named coupling predicate fits the in-scope state it ALSO surfaces a field-threaded carrier (`inv … s1{1} s2{2}`), plus candidate inductive side-conditions (`dom ⊆ used-set`) for any random oracle the call queries. All revisable starting points — confirm the coupling and add/adjust the semantic conjuncts (key/state correspondences, set-membership).

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: A tactic has multiple EasyCrypt argument forms.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch or call tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

ask_manager_for.use_when: LHS/RHS statement order may need swap/alignment context.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier
